contentBlocks.original:
करचरण कृतं वाक्कायजं कर्मजं वा
श्रवणनयनजं वा मानसं वापराधं
विहितमविहितं वा सर्वमेतत्क्षमस्व
जय जय करुणाब्धे श्रीमहादेव शम्भो
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karacaraṇa kṛtaṃ vākkāyajaṃ karmajaṃ vā
śravaṇanayanajaṃ vā mānasaṃ vāparādhaṃ
vihitamavihitaṃ vā sarvametatkṣamasva
jaya jaya karuṇābdhe śrīmahādeva śambho
contentBlocks.translation: O Mahadeva Shambhu (Lord Shiva), who is an ocean of compassion, please forgive all these transgressions - those committed by hands and feet, those arising from speech and body, those born of actions, those originating from hearing and sight, and those of the mind - whether they occurred in performing prescribed duties or prohibited actions. Victory, victory to You!
contentBlocks.meanings: करचरण (karacaraṇa) – hands and feet (kara:hand | caraṇa:foot); कृतं (kṛtaṃ) – done; वाक्कायजं (vākkāyajaṃ) – arising from speech and body (vāk:speech | kāyajaṃ:arising from body); कर्मजं (karmajaṃ) – born of actions; वा (vā) – or; श्रवणनयनजं (śravaṇanayanajaṃ) – originating from hearing and sight (śravaṇa:hearing | nayanajaṃ:originating from sight); वा (vā) – or; मानसं (mānasaṃ) – of the mind; वा (vā) – or; अपराधं (aparādhaṃ) – transgression; विहितम् (vihitam) – prescribed; अविहितं (avihitaṃ) – prohibited; वा (vā) – or; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; एतत् (etat) – this; क्षमस्व (kṣamasva) – forgive; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; करुणाब्धे (karuṇābdhe) – ocean of compassion (karuṇā:compassion | abdhe:ocean); श्रीमहादेव (śrīmahādeva) – O Mahadeva (śrī:O | mahādeva:Mahadeva); शम्भो (śambho) – Shambhu;
